meaning: adj.不可动摇的,坚定不移的
examples: She had an unshakable faith in human goodness and natural honesty.
There was a glow on his face, wrinkled by a life time of suffering, that inspired one with a feeling of unshakable optimism.
His unshakable concentration was among his greatest weapons as a batsman, as was his intelligence.
In my mind, the military has been an institution of honor, justice and unshakable loyalty.
